growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with pyruvate as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: Both Ancylomarina euxinus strain M2P and Labilibaculum euxinus strain SYP grew well on pyruvate (doubling time 1.5-2.0 h; Fig. S20 A, B)
growth_records.notes: Pyruvate doubling time 1.5-2.0 h; midpoint 1.75 h; mu = 0.693/1.75 = 0.396 h-1. Glucose doubling 1.8-2.0 h (mu ~0.365) also reported for SYP (see Ancylomarina record).

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 164274 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/164274
